title: 10 possible side effects of Cinchona officinalis (Cinchona) if used improperly
intro: Cinchona officinalis, also known as Jesuit's bark or Peruvian bark, has been used traditionally for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever and malaria. However, if used improperly, it can cause several health issues. Overdosing on Cinchona officinalis can lead to cinchonism, a set of symptoms including nausea, vomiting, headache, visual disturbances, and in severe cases, ringing in the ears or hearing loss. In extreme cases, it can even cause death due to cardiac arrest or respiratory failure. Pregnant women should avoid it as it can trigger uterine contractions leading to miscarriage. Additionally, long-term use can result in kidney damage or skin disorders. Therefore, it is crucial to consult with a healthcare provider before using Cinchona officinalis for medicinal purposes.
